rationale: The US-Iran ceasefire continuation with oil prices falling is mildly positive for Romanian energy costs, but no new qualitative development beyond last cycle's baseline.

top_events.title: US-Iran ceasefire holds; peace deal signing Friday; oil prices slide
top_events.summary: Continued oil price decline benefits Romania's energy import costs modestly, but no new qualitative leap from last cycle's confirmed ceasefire baseline.

top_events.title: G7 summit protests in Geneva
top_events.summary: Clashes at G7 in Geneva are distant background noise with no direct impact on Romanian daily life.

top_events.title: Lebanese remain sceptical of US-Iran ceasefire
top_events.summary: Regional scepticism about the ceasefire's durability is relevant context for Black Sea security indirectly, but no concrete change for Romania.
